chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Cr. Bail Application No.589 of 2026 ( Areeba Maaz & Another versus The State & Others ) DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs 1. For orders on M.A. No.3106/2026 (U/A) 2. For orders on M.A. No.3107/2026 (Exp/A) 3. For hearing of bail application ------------------------- 23.02.2026 Mr. Sikandar Ayoub Khaskheli, advocate a/w applicants ------------------------------------ 1. Urgency granted. 2. Exemption is granted subject to all just exceptions. 3. Applicants/accused Areeba Maaz wife of Maaz Ali and Muhammad Muttal Imran son of Muhammad Imran, seek protective bail in FIR No.22/2026, registered at P.S. Baiji Sharif, District Sukkur, for offence under Sections 489-F, 420, 406, PPC. Learned counsel for applicants submits that applicants are innocent and they have been falsely implicated in number of cases registered by the same complainant party at various police stations of the Province of Sindh for mala fide intentions and ulterior motives; that they are a law abiding citizen and intend to surrender before the concerned Court but they apprehend their arrest at the hands of concerned police in order to humiliate and disgrace, hence they need protection in order to enable them to appear before the concerned Court. Without touching the merits of the case, protective bail is granted to applicants/accused above named, for a period of seven days from today, subject to their furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.25,000/- (Rupees Twenty Five Thousand) each and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Applicants are directed to surrender before the concerned Court in the aforesaid period and join the trial forthwith.

Without touching the merits of the case, protective bail is granted to applicants/accused above named, for a period of seven days from today, subject to their furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.25,000/- (Rupees Twenty Five Thousand) each and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Applicants are directed to surrender before the concerned Court in the aforesaid period and join the trial forthwith. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of seven days from today or whenever the applicants surrender before the concerned Court, which one occurs earlier. The applicants are cautioned that this concession of protective bail will not be extended for any reason whatsoever and if they fail to surrender himself before the relevant Court during validity of this protective bail, their bail bond shall stand forfeited. Instant Criminal Bail Application stands disposed of in the foregoing terms. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
